title: rosa stylosa var. australis
intro: Rosa stylosa var. australis, also known as the pink rose or Australian rose, is a species of rose that is native to Australia and is known for its fragrant pink flowers and edible hips. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The hips of the Rosa stylosa var. australis plant are rich in vitamins A and C, and have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections. Rosa stylosa var. australis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
